mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls indicates ongoing labor market expansion, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as aggregate income growth supports corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in PAYEMS coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 4.5 percent from below
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal fires fewer than 5 times in a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in unemployment and declines in industrial production generate a joint recession signal that predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through compressed earnings expectations and rising risk premia.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases month-over-month while INDPRO decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: In months where UNRATE rises and INDPRO falls, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or the interaction term lacks statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a pooled regression.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days only when the federal funds rate is below 2 percent, as low financing costs amplify the passthrough from consumption to corporate cash flows; this relationship inverts or vanishes under restrictive monetary policy.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 2%, positive month-over-month RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 5-day S&P 500 returns with rank IC > 0.04 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,200 thousand units signals contraction in residential investment and downstream demand for durable goods, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through reduced construction and consumer spending multipliers.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than half of the months where HOUST is below 1200, or the signal fails to generate a negative mean return with t-test p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in consumer price inflation above 0.5 percent signals overheating that pressures discount rates and margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprice terminal rate expectations.
failure_condition: Months with CPIAUCSL monthly changes above 0.5% do not produce negative average 5-day S&P 500 returns (t-test p > 0.05), or the hit rate of negative returns falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate signals easing monetary conditions that reduce equity discount rates and support positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in FEDFUNDS coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth below 1.0 percent annualized signals demand stagnation that increases recession probability and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings estimates are marked down.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 1.0 percent
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth below 1.0% annualized fail to produce negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal's hit rate for negative returns is below 50%.
